title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Data-System Transfer
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a guarded ticket-mutation workflow with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.
Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Insert Values Select
steps.instruction:
lab case — Import ticket 204 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.
